subject: Mathematics
reference: JEE Main 2026 / National Testing Agency (NTA) / jeemain.nta.nic.in
notes: 30 questions | 120 marks | Section A: 20 MCQs, Section B: 10 Numericals (attempt any 5) | Mathematical Induction and Mathematical Reasoning removed from 2024 onwards

sub_topics: Sets — types, union, intersection, complement, power sets
Venn diagrams and their applications
Relations — types of relations, equivalence relation
Functions — domain, co-domain, range
Types of functions — injective, surjective, bijective
Composition and inverse of functions
Real-valued functions — polynomial, rational, modulus, signum, exponential, logarithmic, greatest integer
Complex numbers — algebra of complex numbers, modulus and argument
Argand diagram and polar form
Square root of a complex number
Triangle inequality
Cube roots of unity
De Moivre's theorem and its applications
Quadratic equations — nature of roots and discriminant
Relation between roots and coefficients
Formation of quadratic equations with given roots
Equations reducible to quadratic form
Types of matrices — row, column, square, diagonal, scalar, identity, zero
Operations on matrices — addition, subtraction, and multiplication
Transpose, adjoint, and inverse of a matrix
Determinant of a square matrix — properties and minors and cofactors
Singular and non-singular matrices
System of linear equations — consistency and Cramer's rule
Solving system of equations using matrix method
Fundamental principle of counting
Factorials and their properties
Permutations — with and without repetition
Combinations — selection problems
Problems involving arrangement and selection
Permutations and combinations applied to geometry (diagonals, triangles from points)
Binomial theorem for positive integral index
General term and middle term in a binomial expansion
Binomial coefficients — Pascal's triangle
Properties of binomial coefficients
Term independent of x
Binomial theorem for any index (conceptual)
Arithmetic progression (AP) — nth term, sum of n terms
Geometric progression (GP) — nth term, sum of n terms, infinite GP
Relationship between AM and GM
Harmonic progression (HP) and harmonic mean
Sum of special series — sum of n natural numbers, sum of squares, sum of cubes
Arithmetic-geometric progression
Limits — definition and properties
Standard limits — algebraic, trigonometric, exponential and logarithmic
L'Hôpital's rule (application)
Continuity — continuity at a point and on an interval
Types of discontinuity
Differentiability — definition and relationship with continuity
Derivatives of standard functions
Rules of differentiation — product rule, quotient rule, chain rule
Derivatives of implicit functions
Derivatives of parametric functions
Higher order derivatives
Rolle's theorem and Lagrange's mean value theorem
Rate of change of quantities
Increasing and decreasing functions
Tangent and normal to a curve
Maxima and minima — local and absolute
First and second derivative tests
Approximation using differentials
Integration as the inverse of differentiation
Standard integrals and properties
Integration by substitution
Integration using partial fractions
Integration by parts
Definite integrals — properties and evaluation
Fundamental theorem of calculus
Area under curves — area between two curves
Applications of definite integrals
Order and degree of a differential equation
Formation of differential equations
Variable separable method
Homogeneous differential equations
Linear differential equations — first order
Integrating factor method
Applications of differential equations — growth, decay, and Newton's cooling law
Cartesian coordinate system — distance formula, section formula, centroid
Slope of a line and angle between two lines
Various forms of equation of a line — slope-intercept, point-slope, intercept, normal form
General form of a line and distance from a point to a line
Angle bisectors and concurrent lines
Family of lines and its applications
Standard and general equations of a circle
Centre and radius from general equation
Position of a point with respect to a circle
Tangent to a circle — length of tangent, condition of tangency
Normal to a circle
Chord of contact
Family of circles — common chord and radical axis
Parabola — standard equations, focus, directrix, vertex, latus rectum
Ellipse — standard equations, major and minor axes, eccentricity, foci, latus rectum
Hyperbola — standard equations, conjugate hyperbola, asymptotes, eccentricity
Tangent and normal to parabola, ellipse, and hyperbola
Chord of contact and polar line
Position of a point relative to conics
Types of vectors — collinear, coplanar, equal, unit vectors
Addition and subtraction of vectors — triangle and parallelogram law
Position vector of a point dividing a line segment
Dot product (scalar product) — properties and geometric interpretation
Cross product (vector product) — properties and geometric interpretation
Triple product — scalar triple product and its applications
Direction cosines and direction ratios of a line
Equation of a line in 3D — Cartesian and vector forms
Angle between two lines
Skew lines — shortest distance between two skew lines
Equation of a plane — general, normal, and intercept forms
Angle between a line and a plane
Angle between two planes
Distance of a point from a plane
Intersection of a line and a plane
Family of planes
Trigonometric functions — definition, domain, range, and graphs
Trigonometric identities and their proofs
Signs and values of trigonometric functions
Periodicity of trigonometric functions
Transformations — sum and product formulae
Trigonometric equations — general solutions
Inverse trigonometric functions — domain, range, and principal values
Properties of inverse trigonometric functions
Applications — solutions of triangles using sine and cosine rules
Measures of dispersion — range, mean deviation, variance, and standard deviation
Analysis of frequency distributions
Probability — random experiment, sample space, events
Axiomatic definition of probability
Addition theorem of probability
Mutually exclusive and exhaustive events
Conditional probability and multiplication theorem
Independent events
Bayes' theorem and its applications
Random variables and probability distributions
Binomial distribution — mean and variance
